title: Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting
content: An incremental rotary encoder outputs two square waves A and B, 90° out of phase. Counting A-rising edges while reading B gives direction: B=LOW on A-rising = clockwise (count++); B=HIGH on A-rising = counter-clockwise (count−−). Mechanical encoders have 20–30 detents/rev; optical/magnetic encoders: 100–1024 pulses/rev.
extended_content: Mechanical rotary encoders (e.g., EC11) include an integral push-switch (press to confirm). Bounce time: 3–10 ms — requires hardware RC debounce (10 nF to GND) or software hysteresis (sample A/B in ISR, confirm only if stable for 5 ms). Typical application: menu navigation — each detent = one menu step; push = select. Pull-up resistors (10 kΩ) required on A, B, and SW pins; most MCU internal pull-ups (40 kΩ) are too weak for reliable reading. KY-040 breakout module includes on-board pull-ups. Position tracking formula: count × (360° / pulses_per_rev) gives angular position. For user interfaces, a state-machine approach (track transitions A/B 00→01→11→10→00 for CW) is more noise-resistant than simple edge counting.
source: ALPS EC11 Rotary Encoder Datasheet; Bourns PEC11 Series Data Sheet; Arduino RotaryEncoder Library
